derivation: A report generator formats stored data into a printed/on-screen report; it does not update files (DML/forms) nor perform data entry.
Data hierarchy: bit -> character -> field -> record -> file. Related fields group to form a record; related records form a file.
DROP is the DDL verb that removes schema objects; DROP FUNCTION removes a stored function. DELETE is DML (rows only); REMOVE/ERASE are not SQL.
SELECT DISTINCT is the standard SQL duplicate-collapsing clause. SELECT UNIQUE is only a legacy Oracle synonym and SELECT DIFFERENT does not exist, so 'All of the above' is false.
ALTER TRIGGER <name> ENABLE|DISABLE is the command targeting an individual trigger. MODIFY TRIGGER does not exist; ALTER DATABASE/TABLE do not target a trigger.
Modification of the database = insertion, deletion, updating (they change stored data). Sorting only orders a result set and changes nothing stored.
A relation = the table itself. DROP TABLE removes rows, structure and schema definition; DELETE removes only rows, ALTER only changes structure, ERASE is not SQL.

Referential integrity constrains the child table: every non-null foreign-key value must match an existing primary-key value in the referenced parent. The reverse direction is not required (a parent row may have no children).
ALTER INDEX is the only real SQL command among the options; it changes an existing index's properties including future storage allocation (ALTER INDEX ... STORAGE / REBUILD).

Data integrity is defined as the correctness/accuracy and completeness of stored data. Security = access control; a constraint is an enforcement mechanism; independence = insulation from schema change.
DML = SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, so retrieval, insertion and deletion are all DML capabilities -> All of the above.
